title: Tandoori Chicken Charcoal Grilled

description: Experience the quintessential taste of India with our authentic Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken. Marinated in a rich blend of yogurt and aromatic spices, this chicken is slowly cooked over hot coals to achieve a smoky, tender perfection with a vibrant red hue. It is a truly irresistible dish that brings the heart of Indian cuisine to your backyard.

ingredients.notes: cut into 8-10 pieces, deep gashes made
hung for 30 minutes to remove excess water

instructions.title: Prepare the Chicken
instructions.text: Wash the chicken pieces thoroughly and pat them completely dry with paper towels. Make 2-3 deep gashes on each piece, especially on thicker parts, to allow the marinade to penetrate deeply. Set aside.

instructions.title: First Marination
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine the chicken pieces with 1 tablespoon of lemon juice, 1/2 teaspoon of salt, and 1 teaspoon of Kashmiri red chili powder. Rub this mixture into the chicken, ensuring it gets into the gashes. Let it rest for 15-20 minutes at room temperature.

instructions.title: Prepare the Second Marinade
instructions.text: In another bowl, whisk together the hung yogurt, remaining ginger-garlic paste, remaining lemon juice, remaining Kashmiri red chili powder, turmeric powder, cumin powder, coriander powder, garam masala, remaining salt, mustard oil, and red food color (if using). Mix until a smooth, thick marinade is formed.

instructions.title: Second Marination (Overnight Recommended)
instructions.text: Add the chicken pieces to the second marinade, ensuring each piece is thoroughly coated. Cover the bowl and refrigerate for at least 4 hours, preferably overnight, for the best flavor and tenderness. The longer the marination, the better the taste.

instructions.title: Prepare the Charcoal Grill
instructions.text: About 30-40 minutes before grilling, prepare your charcoal grill. Arrange the coals for medium-hot direct heat. The coals should be glowing red with a thin layer of ash. Lightly oil the grill grates to prevent sticking.

instructions.title: Grill the Tandoori Chicken
instructions.text: Place the marinated chicken pieces directly on the hot grill grates. Cook for about 25-35 minutes, turning every 5-7 minutes to ensure even cooking and charring. Baste the chicken occasionally with a mixture of melted butter or oil to keep it moist and enhance the flavor. Cook until the chicken is cooked through, tender, and has beautiful charred spots.

instructions.title: Rest and Serve
instructions.text: Once cooked, remove the tandoori chicken from the grill and let it rest for a few minutes. Garnish with fresh chopped cilantro, onion rings, and lemon wedges. Serve hot with mint chutney or a side salad.

tips: Always use hung yogurt for the marinade; this prevents the marinade from becoming too watery and helps it cling better to the chicken.
For the most authentic flavor and tender chicken, marinate the chicken overnight. The longer it marinates, the deeper the flavors will penetrate.

Manage your charcoal heat carefully. Start with medium-high heat for a good sear, then move to a slightly cooler spot if needed to cook through without burning. Baste regularly to keep the chicken moist.
article:
Unlock the secrets to a truly authentic Indian culinary experience right in your backyard with the tantalizing aroma and robust flavor of Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken. This iconic dish, a staple of Indian dinner recipes, is renowned worldwide for its vibrant color, tender meat, and the unmistakable smoky char that only a traditional tandoor oven, or in our case, a charcoal grill, can impart. Forget bland, oven-baked versions; we’re diving deep into creating a masterpiece that will transport your taste buds straight to the streets of Delhi or the finest Indian restaurants. Get ready to impress your family and friends with a dish that is both incredibly delicious and surprisingly achievable at home.

## What is Tandoori Chicken Charcoal Grilled?

Tandoori Chicken is a classic Indian dish where chicken pieces are marinated in yogurt and a blend of aromatic spices, then traditionally cooked in a tandoor – a cylindrical clay oven. The intense heat of the tandoor, combined with the smoky essence of charcoal, gives the chicken its characteristic flavor and slightly charred exterior while keeping the inside remarkably juicy. When we talk about Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken, we’re replicating this traditional method using a standard charcoal grill, aiming for that same authentic smoky depth and beautiful char. The marinade, typically red or orange due to chili powder and often food coloring, tenderizes the meat and infuses it with layers of flavor, making it a star among Indian dinner recipes.

## Ingredients You Need

To embark on this flavorful journey, gather the following:

For the Chicken:
*   1.5 kg (about 3 lbs) whole chicken, cut into 8 pieces (skinless, bone-in preferred)

For the Marinade:
*   1 cup (240g) full-fat plain yogurt, ideally Greek or strained regular yogurt
*   2 tablespoons ginger-garlic paste
*   Juice of 1 large lemon
*   1 teaspoon turmeric powder
*   2 teaspoons Kashmiri red chili powder (for color and mild heat) or regular chili powder (for more heat)
*   1 teaspoon ground cumin
*   1 teaspoon ground coriander
*   1 teaspoon garam masala
*   1.5 teaspoons salt (adjust to taste)
*   1-2 tablespoons mustard oil or vegetable oil (optional, for richness)
*   A few drops of red or orange food coloring (optional, for authentic restaurant color)

For Garnish:
*   Fresh cilantro, chopped
*   Lemon wedges

## Step-by-Step Instructions

Follow these steps to create your perfect Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken:

**Step 1: Prepare the Chicken**
Thoroughly wash the chicken pieces and pat them completely dry with paper towels. Using a sharp knife, make 2-3 deep slits on each piece of chicken. This helps the marinade penetrate deeply and cook more evenly.

**Step 2: First Marinade (Optional but Recommended)**
In a large bowl, combine the chicken pieces with half of the lemon juice and 1 teaspoon of salt. Rub it all over the chicken. Let it sit for 15-20 minutes. This initial marinade helps to tenderize the chicken and kick-starts the flavor infusion. Rinse the chicken pieces lightly and pat dry again.

**Step 3: Prepare the Second Marinade**
In a separate large bowl, combine the yogurt, ginger-garlic paste, remaining lemon juice, turmeric powder, red chili powder, ground cumin, ground coriander, garam masala, and the remaining 0.5 teaspoon of salt. If using, add the mustard oil and food coloring. Mix everything thoroughly until you have a smooth, vibrant paste.

**Step 4: Marinate the Chicken**
Add the chicken pieces to the second marinade, ensuring each piece is fully coated. Use your hands to really work the marinade into the slits. Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for a minimum of 4 hours, or ideally, overnight (up to 24 hours) for the best flavor and tenderness.

**Step 5: Prepare the Grill**
About 30-40 minutes before you plan to cook, prepare your charcoal grill. Arrange the charcoal for medium-high, indirect heat. Once the coals are glowing and covered with a light layer of grey ash, clean the grill grates thoroughly and oil them to prevent sticking.

**Step 6: Grill the Chicken**
Remove the chicken from the refrigerator about 30 minutes before grilling to bring it closer to room temperature. Place the marinated chicken pieces directly over the medium-high heat. Grill for about 20-30 minutes, turning every 5-7 minutes, until the chicken is cooked through, beautifully charred on the outside, and reaches an internal temperature of 165°F (74°C). The yogurt marinade can cause some flare-ups, so keep an eye on it and move pieces to a cooler part of the grill if needed. You can baste the chicken with any leftover marinade (ensure it’s cooked through) or a little melted butter during the last few minutes for extra moisture and shine.

**Step 7: Rest and Serve**
Once cooked, remove the Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken from the grill and let it rest for 5-10 minutes. This allows the juices to redistribute, ensuring tender and moist chicken. Garnish with fresh cilantro and lemon wedges before serving.

## Pro Tips for the Best Results

Achieving that perfect, restaurant-quality Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken requires a few insider tricks:

*   **Quality Yogurt:** Use thick, full-fat plain yogurt. If using regular yogurt, strain it through a cheesecloth for an hour or two to remove excess whey. This prevents the marinade from becoming watery and helps it cling to the chicken better, resulting in a richer crust and less burning.
*   **Don't Skimp on Marination:** The longer the chicken marinates, the more tender and flavorful it will be. Overnight is truly ideal for maximum flavor penetration and moisture retention.
*   **Control Grill Temperature:** While you want high heat for char, direct scorching can burn the marinade before the chicken cooks through. Start with medium-high heat and adjust by moving pieces to cooler zones if they are charring too quickly.
*   **Scoring is Key:** Those deep slits in the chicken aren't just for show; they allow the marinade to penetrate deep into the meat, ensuring flavor in every bite and helping the chicken cook more evenly.
*   **Don't Overcrowd the Grill:** Give each piece of Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken enough space to cook evenly and allow for proper air circulation. This helps achieve a better char and prevents steaming.

## Serving Suggestions

Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken is a complete experience in itself, but it truly shines when paired with traditional Indian accompaniments. Serve it hot with soft, pillowy naan bread or roti to mop up the delicious juices. It’s also excellent with fluffy basmati rice, perhaps a simple jeera rice (cumin rice). A refreshing side of kachumber salad (diced cucumber, tomato, onion with a light dressing) or a cooling mint chutney or raita (yogurt with cucumber and spices) perfectly balances the richness of the chicken. Don't forget extra lemon wedges for a final squeeze of freshness! This makes for an unforgettable Indian dinner recipe.

## Variations and Substitutions

While the classic Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken is a masterpiece, feel free to experiment:

*   **Different Cuts:** While bone-in chicken is traditional, you can use boneless chicken thighs or even breast pieces. Adjust grilling time accordingly, as boneless pieces cook faster.
*   **Vegetarian Options:** The tandoori marinade is incredibly versatile! Apply it to paneer (Indian cheese), mushrooms, bell peppers, or cauliflower florets for delicious Tandoori Paneer Tikka or vegetable skewers.
*   **Spice Level:** Adjust the amount of red chili powder to suit your preference. For more heat, use a spicier variety; for less, reduce the quantity or use a milder paprika.
*   **No Charcoal Grill?** While the smoky flavor of Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken is unparalleled, you can still make a delicious version indoors. Bake in a preheated oven at 400°F (200°C) for 25-35 minutes, then finish under the broiler for a few minutes on each side to get some char. A grill pan can also be used, though it won't impart the same smoky depth.
*   **Smoky Flavor Hack (Indoor):** For an extra smoky kick even without a charcoal grill, heat a small piece of charcoal until red hot. Place it in a small, heat-proof bowl in the center of your marinated chicken (before cooking), drizzle a teaspoon of ghee or oil over the charcoal, and immediately cover the bowl tightly for 5-10 minutes. This "dhungar" method infuses a beautiful smoky aroma.

Bringing the authentic flavors of India to your home kitchen has never been easier or more rewarding. This Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken recipe is more than just food; it's an experience, a celebration of vibrant spices and smoky perfection. So fire up your grill, embrace the intoxicating aromas, and get ready to savor one of the most beloved Indian dinner recipes. Your taste buds will thank you!
